meaning: n.圆石,鹅卵石v.修,拙劣地修补
examples: Soapy took a cobble-stone and dashed it through the glass.
It looks like a large, chipped grey cobble.
Still I managed to cobble a list together.
Most restaurants can cobble a couple of dishes together, but full meals are usually an afterthought.
When it snowed last year, each small, square cobble was like an ice cube.
